body:
## Summary

Issue #3 is a planning/research issue: plan full in/out processing for the top-10 programming languages, top-10 natural languages, and mixed-mode txt/Markdown/HTML; copy competitor test coverage into a migration plan; and compile the supporting data into `docs/case-studies/issue-3/`.

This PR adds that case study folder. It is documentation-only: no runtime or `src/` changes.

After maintainer feedback on PR #4, the planned implementation issues were created with `gh` and are now tracked as real GitHub issues #5 through #20. The source specs and idempotent creation script remain in `docs/case-studies/issue-3/proposed-issues/`.

Closes #3.

## Filed Implementation Issues

| Issue | Area |
|---|---|
| #5 | Plain-text (`txt`) as a first-class container target |
| #6 | Natural-language ordering convention |
| #7 | tree-sitter universal lossless parser front-end |
| #8 | Mixed-mode embedding via tree-sitter injection |
| #9 | Natural-language segmentation and identification |
| #10 | Visual Basic grammar gap |
| #11 | SQL dialect grammars |
| #12 | Delphi/Object Pascal grammar |
| #13 | Substitution/query matcher enrichment |
| #14 | Persistent immutable snapshots |
| #15 | Unified query + transform surface |
| #16 | Self-description roots as links |
| #17 | Common concept ontology |
| #18 | Cross-language reconstruction + configurable formalization |
| #19 | External competitor test corpora |
| #20 | Ecosystem test corpora |

The filed issue bodies were refreshed from the source specs after issue numbers were known, so dependencies now point at real issues (for example, #8, #10, #11, #12, #15, and #19 reference #7).

## What's In The Case Study

| File | What it is |
|---|---|
| `README.md` | Overview, key findings, document index, and filed-issue status. |
| `requirements.md` | Traceable register of every requirement from issue #3 and issue #1. |
| `solution-plans.md` | One solution per requirement cluster, build-vs-reuse decisions, and the six-phase plan. |
| `rust-libraries-survey.md` | Reusable Rust crates and grammar gaps. |
| `competitor-test-suites.md` | External suites, formats, licenses, and adaptation plans. |
| `ecosystem-foundations.md` | link-foundation/linksplatform ecosystem parity and corrected figures. |
| `online-research.md` | TIOBE May 2026, Ethnologue 2025, license safety, and fact-checks. |
| `proposed-issues/` | Source specs for filed issues #5-#20 plus `create-issues.sh`. |
| `raw-data/` | GitHub JSON for issue/PR context plus `implementation-issues-5-20.json`. |

## Key Findings

- The crate is a well-scoped scaffold: public contract and parity tracking exist; real grammar integration, concept ontology work, persistent storage, and corpus adoption remain implementation work.
- `txt` was the one untracked explicit coverage target; it is now tracked by #5.
- Reuse is the preferred path: tree-sitter covers 7/10 programming targets immediately, while Visual Basic, SQL dialects, and Delphi/Object Pascal need separate grammar work.
- External and ecosystem competitor corpora are license-compatible and decomposed into #19 and #20.

## Verification

- Created issues #5-#20 with `./docs/case-studies/issue-3/proposed-issues/create-issues.sh --create`.
- Re-ran the script in dry-run mode: 0 would create, 16 skipped as existing.
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `cargo test --all-features` (71 passed)
- `rust-script scripts/check-no-src-tests.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-crate-size.rs` (crate archive 0.06 MiB)
- `git diff --check`
title: Plan full implementation for top 10 programming and top 10 natural languages, as well as full support for txt, markdown and html in mixed mode
